reasoning: The Federal Reserve cut rates by 25 basis points on December 10, 2025, lowering the target range to 3.50% - 3.75%. The SOFR 30-day average (SOFR30DAYAVG) operates as a 30-calendar-day moving average of daily SOFR rates. Because daily rates dropped discretely around Dec 11, the 30-day average declines mechanically and continuously for 30 days as higher pre-cut rates roll out of the window. On the forecast date (2025-12-21), this mechanical decline is only about 10 days in, meaning the value (~3.89) will continue to decrease for roughly another 20 days until it reaches its new steady state (~3.6-3.7). Therefore, by Dec 28 it will be significantly lower (~3.82). By Jan 20, 2026, it will have reached the new, lower steady state range safely below the 2025-12-21 value. By Mar 21, 2026, absent two unexpected 25-bps rate hikes, it will remain below the 2025-12-21 baseline value. The probabilities are set at the lower bound given the mathematical certainty of the immediate decline and macro expectations indicating further cuts rather than hikes in early 2026.
